methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $135M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpliceBio is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $135M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M), alongside 3 public sector comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $135M total raised as a proxy for current valuation, yielding $256M. Peer-median valuation across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (6.6x median across the 3 public tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of SpliceBio's revenue data, typical for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $218M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $533M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion within the peer set—a 5.5x range from floor to ceiling—and the limited applicability of stage-progression anchoring to early-stage biotech valuations. Key uncertainties include SpliceBio's clinical stage, intellectual property scope, and burn profile relative to peers. Revenue visibility would enable public-comp calibration and narrow the range materially.
